Now let's look at a simple grammar idea that ties everything together.
In Russian, every noun has a gender.
Think of it like belonging to a team.
The team changes a little word like my.
Most nouns ending in a consonant are masculine.
So we say, Мой учитель, and Мой друг, and Мой урок.
Most nouns ending in A or Y are feminine.

Want to know a secret about книга?
Did you know that the Russian word книга and the English word book are actually distant
They both trace back to ancient words connected to beach trees.
The very first books were written on thin slices of beach wood.
The idea started in old languages, traveled through old English as bok meaning beach, while
a similar word for written record came into Slavic languages as книга around the 10th century
